skill.name: google-agents-cli-scaffold
skill.description: Guides agents through Google ADK project scaffolding with agents-cli. It covers create, enhance, and upgrade workflows, template selection, deployment targets, CI/CD options, and prototype-first setup.
skill.summary: Scaffold and enhance Google ADK agent projects with agents-cli guidance.
skill.author: google

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell execution, weak crypto, filesystem, network, and credential-access patterns. Review found Markdown CLI examples, flag tables, an install URL, /tmp reference scaffolds, and a .env customization note, with no executable code, prompt injection attempt, or evidence of exfiltration. The remaining risk is low because the skill guides project-generation commands that can create or modify files after user confirmation.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Can Modify Projects
low_findings.description: The skill documents agents-cli scaffold commands that create, enhance, or upgrade agent projects. This is expected functionality, but it can write files in the target project, so user confirmation and path review are important.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are explicit CLI examples for scaffolding operations. They are legitimate documentation, but their purpose is to run commands that can create or modify files.

low_findings.title: False Positive Static Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and heuristic combination alerts appear to come from Markdown backticks, words such as agent and session, flag names, and documentation tables. No executable Ruby, cryptographic implementation, system reconnaissance command, prompt injection, or covert network behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanned files are Markdown guidance and flag references. Semantic review found documentation syntax and product terminology, not executable code or malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Environment File Mention Requires Secret Handling Care
low_findings.description: The skill points users to .env as a file to customize for project IDs, locations, and API keys. It does not instruct the agent to read or exfiltrate secrets, but generated projects should avoid exposing this file in logs or commits.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The .env reference is explicit and includes API keys, but the context is a customization checklist. No evidence found that the skill reads, prints, or transmits environment secrets.

content.value_statement: Starting a Google ADK agent project requires many architecture and deployment choices. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through agents-cli scaffold workflows with clear flags and safety checks.
actual_capabilities: Maps agent architecture choices to agents-cli scaffold flags.
Explains create, enhance, and upgrade workflows for ADK projects.
Covers deployment targets including Agent Runtime, Cloud Run, GKE, and none.
Guides CI/CD runner selection for GitHub Actions or Google Cloud Build.
Documents prototype-first setup before adding deployment infrastructure.
References full create and enhance flag options from references/flags.md.
limitations: Does not write agent business logic or custom tool code.
Does not perform live deployment operations or cloud provisioning by itself.
Requires agents-cli and uv to be installed before commands can run.
Needs user decisions for project name, architecture, deployment target, and CI/CD runner.

use_cases.title: Create a first ADK prototype
use_cases.description: Start a small agent project with the prototype workflow before adding infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Add deployment scaffolding
use_cases.description: Enhance an existing project with Agent Runtime, Cloud Run, or GKE deployment files.

use_cases.title: Prepare a RAG agent project
use_cases.description: Choose the agentic RAG template and the correct Agent Platform search datastore.

prompt_templates.title: Create a prototype agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Google ADK prototype agent project. Ask me the required setup questions before running scaffold commands.

prompt_templates.title: Choose architecture flags
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the correct agents-cli scaffold flags for a RAG agent that may later deploy to Agent Runtime.

prompt_templates.title: Enhance an existing project
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current ADK project layout and guide me through adding Cloud Run deployment scaffolding with agents-cli.

prompt_templates.title: Upgrade and preserve changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an agents-cli scaffold upgrade for this customized ADK project. Include a dry-run step and conflict review process.

output_examples.input: I want a basic ADK agent prototype named support-agent.
output_examples.output: A short requirements checklist, a validated project name, and a confirmed scaffold plan for a prototype ADK project.

output_examples.input: Add production deployment to my prototype.
output_examples.output: A comparison of supported deployment targets, the questions to answer, and the selected enhance workflow after confirmation.

output_examples.input: I need a RAG agent with vector search.
output_examples.output: A recommended agent template, datastore selection, region notes, and follow-up steps for datastore provisioning and ingestion.

best_practices: Clarify requirements and project scope before running scaffold create.
Start with prototype mode, then add deployment and CI/CD when the agent works.
Confirm target directories and agent code location before enhancing a project.
anti_patterns: Do not pre-create the project directory before scaffold create.
Do not choose a CI/CD runner or deployment target without user confirmation.
Do not commit .env files or expose generated project secrets in logs.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create or enhance Google ADK agent project scaffolds with agents-cli.

faq.question: Does it write agent code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on scaffolding, project structure, deployment options, and upgrade guidance.

faq.question: Can it add CI/CD files?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides agents-cli enhance workflows for GitHub Actions or Google Cloud Build after user confirmation.

faq.question: Which deployment targets are covered?
faq.answer: It covers Agent Runtime, Cloud Run, GKE, and no-deployment scaffold options.

faq.question: Is prototype mode supported?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill recommends prototype-first setup for faster iteration before deployment scaffolding.

faq.question: What tools must be installed?
faq.answer: The local environment needs agents-cli, usually installed through uv, before scaffold commands can run.
